groups.title: GOODBYE PARTY FOR JOHN
groups.desc:
Questions 1-10
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS OR A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect: Example                                                       Answer Data                                                             22ⁿᵈ.December Venue: $21912$     Invitations (Tony)     Who to invite:                                           --John and his wife                                                                      --Director                                                                      --The $21913$                                                                      --All the teachers                                                                      --All the $21914$     Date for sending invitations:                      $21915$     Present (Lisa)     Collect money during the:                          $21916$     Suggested amount per person:                  $$21917$     Check prices for:                                      --CD Players                                                                      --$21918$                                                                      --Coffee maker     Ask guests to bring:                                 --snacks                                                                      --$21919$                                                                      --$21920$     Ask student representative to prepare a:   $21921$

questions.analysis: 题干问的是场地（Venue），在听力原文中，丽莎提到“酒店可能会比较贵”，然后她说“我一直在看学院餐厅；那似乎相当合理”。这表明他们最终选择了“学院餐厅”作为场地。因此，正确答案是“college dining room”。
答案是“office staff”，因为在听力材料中，Tony和Lisa在讨论邀请名单时明确提到了“the office Staff”。通过定位词“the”和信号词“the office Staff”，我们可以准确找到答案。
题干中的定位词是“all”，在听力原文中对应的信号词是“all the teachers and all the students”。通过这个信号词，考生可以确定答案是“students”。因为题干要求填入“所有的”某些人或物，而在原文中明确提到“all the students”，所以答案是“students”。
正确答案是“10 December”。在听力原文中，丽莎和托尼讨论了发送邀请函的最佳日期。丽莎最初提议12月15日，但托尼指出16号有考试，因此他们决定避开这个日期。最终，他们确定12月10日是合适的日期。因此，正确答案是“10 December”。
答案是“coffee break”，因为在听力原文中明确提到“we usually go round with an envelope during coffee break”，这表明他们通常在咖啡休息时间收集钱。题干中的“Collect money”是定位词，对应的信号词是“go round with an envelope”，通过这些信息可以确定答案。
在听力原文中，托尼提到“我们上次建议六美元，可以吗？”，这直接回答了题干中“Suggested amount per person”的问题。因此，正确答案是“6”。

questions.answer: set of dictionaries / dictionaries / a good dictionary
questions.analysis: 答案是“set of dictionaries”，因为在听力材料中，LISA和TONY讨论了要查找价格的物品，先提到CD Players，然后TONY提到“a set of dictionaries”。通过抓住定位词“Check prices for”和信号词“check on prices for”，可以准确找到答案。学生需要特别注意听力材料中提到的物品顺序，并且理解相关的词汇和短语。
答案是“music”，因为在听力原文中，LISA明确提到“some music”作为客人需要带的物品之一。考生需要注意定位词“Ask guests to bring”和信号词“bring”，并在听到这些词时特别关注接下来的内容。
答案是“photos”，因为听力原文明确提到“ask the guests to bring photos”，并且Tony还说“I'll put it on the invitations”，这表明“photos”是需要填写的内容。通过定位词“bring”和信号词“ask the guests to bring”，可以准确找到答案。
这道题的正确答案是“speech”，因为听力材料中明确提到学生代表需要准备的是一个演讲。通过定位词“student representative”和信号词“one of the students, Student Leader”，我们可以迅速定位到答案所在的句子。即使基础较差的学生，也可以通过抓住关键词和利用上下文来猜测正确答案。

transcript.en: LISA:Hi TONY, thanks ever so much for coming.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：你好，托尼。非常感谢你能来。

transcript.en: You know we've been asked to organize something for Johns farewell?
transcript.cn: 人们想让我们准备约翰的欢送会？

transcript.en: TONY:Yeah, sure, it's about time we started working out details.
transcript.cn: 托尼：是啊，当然，我们是时候该解决细节问题了。

transcript.en: LISA:Exactly.We don't want to leave it so late that it's double the work.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：没错，我们不想拖太久，最后增加工作量。

transcript.en: TONY:Mmm, mm.right, do you want me to take notes?
transcript.cn: 托尼：好的，你想让我记笔记吗？

transcript.en: LISA:That'd be great, thanks.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：这样太好了，谢谢。

transcript.en: TONY:Right, first thing is, when is the best time to hold it?
transcript.cn: 托尼：好的，首先是举行的最佳时间是什么？

transcript.en: LISA:Well, he leaves on the twenty-fourth of December.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：他在12月24日离开。

transcript.en: TONY:So what about the twenty-second?
transcript.cn: 托尼：那22日怎么样？

transcript.en: LISA:Yeah.I think that's about right.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：好的，我觉得很合适。

transcript.en: We want it quite near the time, don't we?
transcript.cn: 我们要在他快走的时候举行，不是吗？

transcript.en: TONY:Sure, and what about a venue?
transcript.cn: 托尼：对。场地呢？

transcript.en: In college?A hotel?
transcript.cn: 在大学还是酒店举行？

transcript.en: LISA:I think a hotel will probably work out rather expensive, and I've been looking at the College Dining Room; that seems pretty reasonable.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：我认为酒店可能太贵了，学校的餐厅比较合适，价钱好像很合理。

transcript.en: TONY:Fine, yeah, why not?
transcript.cn: 托尼：好的，为什么不呢？

transcript.en: LISA:And then we ought to be thinking about invitations ... Who mustn't we forget to invite?
transcript.cn: 丽莎：之后我们应该想想邀请函的事了，谁是我们必须不能忘记请的？

transcript.en: TONY:Well, obviously John and his wife.
transcript.cn: 托尼：显然是约翰和他的妻子。

transcript.en: LISA:Right.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：没错。

transcript.en: TONY:And the Director.
transcript.cn: 托尼：还有主任。

transcript.en: LISA:Ah ha.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：啊哈。

transcript.en: TONY:The office Staff.
transcript.cn: 托尼：办公室工作人员。

transcript.en: LISA:Yep, and all the teachers and all the students.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：是的，还有所有的老师和学生。

transcript.en: TONY:Anyone else?
transcript.cn: 托尼：还有谁？

transcript.en: LISA:Faculty Heads?
transcript.cn: 丽莎：系里的领导？

transcript.en: TONY:No.better draw the line.
transcript.cn: 托尼：不，最好就到这里了，

transcript.en: I don't think it's necessary.
transcript.cn: 我觉得没必要。

transcript.en: LISA:Yeah, you're right.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：是的，你是对的。

transcript.en: TONY:I don't mind writing the invitations.
transcript.cn: 托尼：我不介意写邀请信。

transcript.en: When shall we get them out for?
transcript.cn: 我们什么时候发出去？

transcript.en: LISA:Enough time but not too early.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：时间很充足，不要太早了。

transcript.en: What about the fifteenth of December?
transcript.cn: 12月15日怎么样？

transcript.en: TONY:Well, there are exams on the sixteenth - better avoid them.
transcript.cn: 托尼：16日有考试，最好错过它们。

transcript.en: LISA:Tenth?
transcript.cn: 丽莎：10号？

transcript.en: TONY:Yeah, that should do it.
transcript.cn: 托尼：好的，就这天了。

transcript.en: LISA:So what does that leave?
transcript.cn: 丽莎：那么还剩下什么？

transcript.en: Oh yes, a present
transcript.cn: 对了，礼物。

transcript.en: TONY:Would you mind doing that?
transcript.cn: 托尼：你介意做这个吗？

transcript.en: LISA:No.not at all; we usually go round with an envelope during coffee break, don't we?
transcript.cn: 丽莎：不介意。我们经常在咖啡时间拿着信封走来走去，不是吗？

transcript.en: TONY:Yeah, coffee breaks always the best time, 'cos people have got their money handy.
transcript.cn: 托尼：是的，喝咖啡的休息时间很合适，因为人们手边正好有钱。

transcript.en: LISA:Yeah, exactly.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：没错。

transcript.en: Do we suggest an amount?
transcript.cn: 我们得要求数额吗？

transcript.en: Or does it seem a bit unfair?
transcript.cn: 或者这么做是不是有点不公平？

transcript.en: TONY:No.I think people welcome it.
transcript.cn: 托尼：没事，我觉得人们不介意。

transcript.en: We suggested six dollars last time, is that OK?
transcript.cn: 我们上次建议交6美元，这样可以吗？

transcript.en: LISA:Yeah, plenty I would have thought, which should leave us with about ninety dollars.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：可以，我觉得足够了，这样我们应该就有90美元了？

transcript.en: TONY:Have you any ideas for presents?
transcript.cn: 托尼：你想好买什么礼物了吗？

transcript.en: LISA:Well, I've been having a little think.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：我有一点想法，

transcript.en: I thought, you know, he loves music.
transcript.cn: 你知道，我想他喜欢音乐。

transcript.en: TONY:Yeah, and books.
transcript.cn: 托尼：是的，还有书。

transcript.en: LISA:So, I thought I'd check on prices for, well, perhaps CD players.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：所以我想根据价钱也许CD播放器合适？

transcript.en: TONY:Yeah, that's a, that's a good idea, and also I thought maybe, you know, a set of dictionaries.
transcript.cn: 托尼：好的，好主意，我还觉得一套字典也不错。

transcript.en: I heard him say he needed a good one.
transcript.cn: 我听他说他想要一套好字典。

transcript.en: LISA:The other thing he was saying last week was that his computer printer had broken.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：上周他还说过他的电脑打印机坏了。

transcript.en: TONY:Umm.No.I'd be really frightened about getting the wrong type.
transcript.cn: 托尼：不，我怕买到错误的机型。

transcript.en: LISA:OK, yeah.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：好的。

transcript.en: TONY:The other thing is something for the home - Jill suggested a coffee maker.
transcript.cn: 托尼：另一件是给家里预备的，吉尔建议买咖啡机。

transcript.en: LISA:Oh yeah.I'll certainly find out what they cost.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：好的，我肯定会去看看它们的价格。

transcript.en: OK, have you got all that down?
transcript.cn: 好的，你都记下了吗？

transcript.en: TONY:Yes.
transcript.cn: 托尼：是的。

transcript.en: LISA:Now we need to think a little more about the money.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：不，我们得再考虑一下资金问题。

transcript.en: I know we've got a set amount from the Social Fund.
transcript.cn: 我知道我们已经从社会筹资上得到了一部分钱。

transcript.en: TONY:Right, what does that cover?
transcript.cn: 托尼：是的，都能包括什么？

transcript.en: LISA:It's meant to cover the cost of the room.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：这包括房间的花费。

transcript.en: TONY:Yeah.
transcript.cn: 托尼：好的。

transcript.en: LISA:And a certain amount for food.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：还包括一部分食物。

transcript.en: TONY:And also drinks?
transcript.cn: 托尼：也包括酒水吗？

transcript.en: LISA:Oh yeah, certainly.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：当然。

transcript.en: TONY:But will it be enough?
transcript.cn: 托尼：这是不是就够了？

transcript.en: LISA:What we've done in the past is to ask guests to bring some snacks
transcript.cn: 丽莎：过去我们是让客人自己带一些点心来。

transcript.en: TONY:Right.
transcript.cn: 托尼：对

transcript.en: LISA:We don't ask them to bring more drinks because we figure that's ... that should come from the Social Fund.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：我们不要求他们带酒水来。因为我们认为这些应该出自社会筹款的部分。

transcript.en: TONY:OK.Anything else for the guests to bring?
transcript.cn: 托尼：好的，还有什么要求客人带来的吗？

transcript.en: LISA:Well, some music, because there'll be a tape deck there in the room, and we can have some dancing later on.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：一些音乐。因为房间里有录音机，我们之后可以跳跳舞。

transcript.en: TONY:Anything else?
transcript.cn: 托尼：还有什么要准备的吗？

transcript.en: LISA:Well, it's just a thought, but a couple of years ago we had a really good party where we set up, you know, some simple games.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：只是一个想法，但是几年前我们举办过一个很棒的聚会，我们会设置一些小游戏。

transcript.en: TONY:Yeah, great. Wasn't it based on photos, from the students and teachers?
transcript.cn: 托尼：太好了。是不是根据师生的照片？

transcript.en: LISA:That's right.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：没错。

transcript.en: TONY:So we should ask the guests to bring photos.
transcript.cn: 托尼：那么我们应该要求客人带照片来，

transcript.en: OK.I'll put it on the invitations.
transcript.cn: 好的，我把这点写在邀请信上。

transcript.en: LISA:Now the last thing is, who shall we ask to do the speech?
transcript.cn: 丽莎：最后的事情是我们请谁来发表演讲？

transcript.en: TONY:Don't you think it might be nice to have one of the students?
transcript.cn: 托尼：你不觉得让其中某位学生来做会很好吗？

transcript.en: LISA:Well then, the Student Leader?
transcript.cn: 丽莎：那么，学生干部怎样？

transcript.en: TONY:Yeah, much better than the Director giving speeches again.
transcript.cn: 托尼：是的，这比主任再次发表演讲要好得多。

transcript.en: LISA:OK then, I'll ask her.
transcript.cn: 丽莎：好吧，我会问问她。

transcript.en: Lovely!So, is that all?
transcript.cn: 不错，就这么多了吧？

transcript.en: TONY:Looks like it.
transcript.cn: 托尼：看起来是的。

transcript.en: LISA:Great.Thanks ever so much... (fade out)
transcript.cn: 丽莎：太棒了，很感谢你......（淡出）

groups.desc:
Questions 11-15
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: To find out how much holidays cost, you should press button
questions.analysis: 题干问的是如何找到假期费用的信息。听力原文中明确提到，如果想听到最新的价格列表，请按二。因此，正确答案是B。题干中的“holidays cost”在原文中被同义替换为“price lists”，这就是我们需要关注的信号词。

questions.title: Travelite currently offer walking holidays
questions.analysis: 这道题的关键在于准确听出Travelite目前提供的walking holidays的地理范围。通过定位词“Travelite”和“walking holidays”，以及信号词“Western Europe”，可以准确找到答案句并确定正确答案为A。学生需要特别注意地理位置的描述，以避免误选。

questions.title: The walks offered by Travelite

options.title: A
options.content: cater for a range of walking abilities

options.title: B
options.content: are planned by guides from the local area

options.title: C
options.content: are for people with good fitness levels

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是A，因为听力材料明确提到“Each day we offer three separate walks catering for all skills and fitness levels”，这表明Travelite的步行活动适合各种技能和体能水平的人。通过抓住关键词和信号词，学生可以更容易地定位到正确答案。

questions.title: On Travelite holidays, people holidaying alone pay

options.title: B
options.content: only a little more than other clients

options.title: C
options.content: extra only if they stay in a large room

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是A，因为原文明确表示单独旅行的人不会支付更多费用。通过抓住定位词“people holidaying alone”和信号词“single client”，并注意否定词“no”，可以准确定位到答案句并选择正确答案。

questions.title: Entertainment is provided
questions.analysis: 正确答案是B“most nights”，因为“nearly every night”与“most nights”意思相近，表示大多数晚上都有娱乐活动。选项C“every night”是不正确的，因为它表示每晚都有娱乐活动，而原文中“nearly”表示“几乎”，并不是每晚都有。选项A“when guests request it”也不正确，因为材料中明确提到娱乐活动是“nearly every night”安排的，而不是根据客人的请求安排的。

groups.desc:
Questions 16-20
Complete the table below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS OR A NUMBER.

content:
As above plus
– book of $21929$
– maps
as above plus membership of a $21931$

questions.analysis: 答案是“180”，因为听力原文明确提到“the three-day holiday costs one hundred and eighty dollars”，并且题目要求填写的内容不能超过三个单词或一个数字。通过定位词“3 days”和信号词“three-day holiday, costs”，可以迅速找到答案所在的句子。对于基础较差的学生，抓住数字和关键词是关键，避免错填为“one hundred and eighty”或“$180”。
本题的正确答案是“nearest station”，因为听力原文明确提到“the cost of picking you up from the nearest station”。考生需要注意定位词“pick up”，并在听力中找到对应的信号词“picking you up”，从而确定答案。对于基础较差的学生，建议在听力前先浏览题目，找出关键词，并在听力过程中特别关注这些关键词及其同义表达。
答案是“local history”，因为听力原文明确提到七天假期的特别优惠包括一本关于当地历史的书。题目要求填写书的内容，而不是书的描述，因此正确答案是“local history”。

第19题的正确答案是“690”，因为听力原文明确提到“the fourteen-day holiday, our special price is six hundred and ninety dollars per person”。通过定位词“14 days”和信号词“fourteen-day holiday”，可以准确找到答案句并提取出答案词“six hundred and ninety dollars”。学生在听力过程中应特别注意这些定位词和信号词，抓住关键数字和相关信息，即使基础较差，也可以通过这些策略提高正确率。
答案是“walking club”，因为听力材料明确提到“membership of a local walking club”。考生需要注意定位词“14 days”和“membership”，并在听力过程中关注这些词汇的出现。通过抓住关键词和利用表格结构，即使基础较差的学生也能较好地应对这道题。

transcript.en: SPEAKER 1:Thank you for calling the free Travelite Travel Agency Information Line.
transcript.cn: 发言人1：感谢你往Travelite旅行社拨打免费的咨询电话，

transcript.en: You will not be charged for this call.
transcript.cn: 这条通话将不收取费用。

transcript.en: In order to deal with all calls effectively, we offer you a number of options.
transcript.cn: 为了更有效的处理每条通话，我们为你提供几个选择。

transcript.en: Please listen carefully and press your required number at the appropriate time, or dial a new number.
transcript.cn: 请仔细听好并在适当的时间按下相应的号码，或拨打新号码。

transcript.en: If you want to hear about special offers, please press one.
transcript.cn: 如果你想了解特价活动，请拨一。

transcript.en: If you want to hear our latest price lists, please press two.
transcript.cn: 如果你想了解我们最新的报价单，请拨二。

transcript.en: If you want to make a complaint, please press three.
transcript.cn: 如果你想投诉，请拨三。

transcript.en: If you want information about our new walking holidays, please press four now.
transcript.cn: 如果你想了解我们的徒步旅行，现在请拨四。

transcript.en: SPEAKER 2:Thank you for calling our Travelite Walking Holidays Line.
transcript.cn: 发言人2：感谢你往Travelite徒步旅行拨打电话。

transcript.en: We have been offering a wide variety of walking holidays to suit all tastes for just three years, but already we have won two awards for excellence in this field.
transcript.cn: 三年我们为不同爱好者提供了各种各样的徒步旅行，在这一领域我们已经取得了两项卓越奖。

transcript.en: We offer guided walking tours to suit the discerning traveller in twelve different centres throughout the whole of Western Europe.
transcript.cn: 我们在遍布整个西欧的12家不同的中心为独具慧眼的旅行者提供适合的徒步旅行指导。

transcript.en: We are planning to open our first centre outside this area in the coming year, so watch out for developments.
transcript.cn: 我们明年计划在这些地方以外开辟首家中心，所以请关注我们的发展。

transcript.en: But the pride of Travelite is the level of guidance and support we offer on our walks.
transcript.cn: 但是Travelite骄傲的是我们提供给顾客最好的指导和支持。

transcript.en: All are planned in detail by our highly trained guides, who all work in a variety of different Travelite locations, so we can guarantee standards.
transcript.cn: 一切都由我们受过高度训练的指导员详细安排，他们在Travelite的各个地区工作，所以我们的水准可以受到保证。

transcript.en: Each day we offer three separate walks catering for all skills and fitness levels.
transcript.cn: 每天我们提供3种不同的徒步计划来迎合各种技能和健康水平的人。

transcript.en: We also pride ourselves on our friendly service, particularly important for the increasing numbers of people who choose to holiday alone.
transcript.cn: 我们还为自己热情的服务感到自豪，这特别是对越来越多只身度假的人很重要。

transcript.en: Unlike almost all travel operators who happily charge large supplements for single rooms, we guarantee that no single client will pay more, even when only double rooms are available for them.
transcript.cn: 我们不像其他所有旅行机构一样对使用单人房的人索取高额费用，

transcript.en: And the day doesn't end with the return to base … after our dinner at communal tables designed to make all our guests feel part of a family atmosphere … Entertainment is laid on nearly every night with tour leaders on hand to organise lectures, games, quizzes and respond to any special requests from guests.
transcript.cn: 并且我们的行程不是在回到基地那天就终止了。在公共餐桌吃完晚餐后让所有客人都能感受到家庭的气氛，几乎每晚我们的领队都会安排娱乐节目，讲座、游戏、竞答和回应客人的特殊要求。

transcript.en: The following is a summary of costs and special inclusive offers on holidays for the coming summer.
transcript.cn: 接下来的是这个即将到来的夏季假期成本和特殊报价的总结。

transcript.en: We have three lengths of holiday: three-day, seven-day and fourteen-day.
transcript.cn: 我们有三种时间跨度的假期：3天、7天、14天。

transcript.en: The three-day holiday costs one hundred and eighty dollars for all accommodation, food and walking, and for the first time this year we are including in that price... the cost of picking you up from the nearest station.
transcript.cn: 三天的假期需要180美元，其中包括食宿、徒步费用，并且我们今年第一次涵盖了从最近的车站接你的费用。

transcript.en: The seven-day holiday costs three hundred and fifty dollars per person and, as well as including the offers of the three-day holiday, also includes a magnificent book giving the local history.
transcript.cn: 七天的假期每人需要350美元，除了包括三天假期的内容外，还送你一本精美的介绍当地历史的书。

transcript.en: On top of that, we are able to include free maps ... for you to better enjoy the walking and even plan in advance if you wish.
transcript.cn: 不仅如此，我们还有免费地图。如果你愿意，你可以更好的享受徒步旅行甚至提前做好计划。

transcript.en: For the fourteen-day holiday, our special price is six hundred and ninety dollars per person and that includes all the offers for the three- and seven- day holidays plus … membership of a local walking club ... so you can better enjoy the full flavour of the local life.
transcript.cn: 我们十四天假期的特价是每人690美元，这不仅包括三天和七天的内容，还包括当地徒步俱乐部的会员资格。这样你就能更好的享受各种各样的当地生活。

transcript.en: For further information, please contact your local travel agent.
transcript.cn: 想要了解更多信息，请联系当地旅行社。

transcript.en: Thank you for calling the Travelite Travel Agency Information Line ... (fade out)
transcript.cn: 感谢你致电Travelite旅行社咨询电话......（淡出）

groups.desc:
Questions 21-26
Complete the table below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS OR A NUMBER.

content: to show how things move on a cushion of air
to show why we need standard $21933$
to teach children about how $21937$is made up
a drill, an old record, a pin, paper, a bolt
to make a record player in order to learn about recording sound

questions.analysis: 第21题的正确答案是“20 balloons”。在听力原文中，明确提到第一个实验需要“twenty balloons and a table”。题干中已经包含了“table”，因此只需要填入“20 balloons”。通过定位词“Experiment 1”可以快速找到相关的部分，而信号词“first experiment”进一步确认了答案的位置。

questions.answer: units of measurement / measurements / measurement units
questions.analysis: 第22题的正确答案是“units of measurement”。题干中的定位词是“standard”，在听力原文中对应的信号词是“fixed”。通过这两个词的对应关系，可以在原文中找到答案句“It's supposed to demonstrate the importance of having fixed units of measurement.”，从中提取出正确的答案词“units of measurement”。
第23题的正确答案是“rock salt”。通过定位词“Experiment 3”以及信号词“number three”，可以在听力原文中找到对应的答案句。听力中提到的设备有“rock salt or copper sulphate”和“a jar of water”，但题干中已经明确提到“and a jar of water”，所以需要填入的是“rock salt”。

第24题的正确答案是“crystals”。通过听力原文中的关键句子“you dissolve lots of salt into the water and watch the crystals form, so it basically teaches the kids about growing crystals”，可以明确“crystals”是正确答案。学生在听力过程中应重点关注与“grow”相关的词汇，并通过记笔记和回忆来推断答案。避免错填“salt”或“water”，因为这些词并不符合题目要求的“how 【24】grow”。
第25题的正确答案是“string”，因为听力原文明确提到需要用“string”来完成实验，并且SUE在对话中确认了这一点。通过抓住定位词“cardboard”和“colored pens”，可以准确定位到答案所在的句子，并从中提取出正确的答案“string”。
第26题的正确答案是“light”，因为听力材料中明确提到实验的目的是教孩子们了解白光的组成。题干中的定位词是“how ... is made up”，在听力原文中对应的信号词是“how white light or ordinary light is made up”。通过上下文可以推测出正确答案。

groups.title: Problems
groups.desc:
Questions 27-30
What problems do the speakers identify for each experiment?
Choose your answers from the box and write the letters A-H next to questions 27-30.

questions.title: Experiment 1
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是 H. too dangerous，因为原文明确提到这个实验存在风险，可能会导致孩子们受伤。通过抓住关键词“risky”和“get hurt”，并结合上下文理解，可以准确定位到答案。

questions.title: Experiment 2
questions.analysis: 正确答案是 B "too boring"，因为听力原文中明确提到实验 2 "tame"（无聊），需要更有趣和活跃的实验。

questions.title: Experiment 3
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是E. too long。通过分析听力原文，可以发现实验3需要等待两周才能看到结果，而学生只有一两个小时的时间，这显然不合适。因此，答案是E. too long。

questions.title: Experiment 5
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是C. too difficult。通过对听力原文的分析，可以看出“ambitious”和“can't cope with it”这两个词汇直接表明了实验的难度过高，不适合八岁的孩子们。因此，选择C是正确的。

transcript.en: MIKE:Hi SUE
transcript.cn: 迈克：你好，休。

transcript.en: SUE:Hi MIKE, so what happened to you last week?
transcript.cn: 苏：你好，迈克，上周你怎么了？

transcript.en: MIKE:Oh, I was sick with the flu.
transcript.cn: 迈克：我得了流感。

transcript.en: What's this I hear about a big assignment we've got to do?
transcript.cn: 我听说我们有一个重要的作业要做？

transcript.en: SUE:Well, basically, we've got to find two science experiments to do with a group of eight-year-old children at the local primary school, and we've got to complete it by the end of the week.
transcript.cn: 苏：我们要找到两个与当地小学的一组8岁孩子有关的科学实验，并且在周末前完成。

transcript.en: MIKE:Oh, that sounds like hard work.
transcript.cn: 迈克：听起来很难。

transcript.en: Where are we supposed to get the ideas for these experiments from?
transcript.cn: 我们应该从哪儿找到实验的灵感呢？

transcript.en: SUE:Well.I managed to get hold of two books from the library.
transcript.cn: 苏：我设法从图书馆拿到了两本书。

transcript.en: MIKE:Oh, well done!
transcript.cn: 迈克：干得好！

transcript.en: SUE:How about if we take a look at the experiments in this book first and see if anything looks suitable?
transcript.cn: 苏：我们先看看这本书上的实验，看看是否有合适的怎么样？

transcript.en: I can make notes as we go, about equipment and the purpose of the experiments.
transcript.cn: 当我们处理设备和实验目的时，我可以记笔记。

transcript.en: MIKE:OK, let's see, um, the first experiment is called 'Make your own hovercraft', which sounds very ambitious!
transcript.cn: 迈克：好的，让我看看，第一个实验叫“自制气垫船”，听起来真有野心！

transcript.en: Mind you, you only need twenty balloons and a table - you don't need any special engines or anything like that!
transcript.cn: 注意，你只需要20个气球和一张桌子，你不需要任何特殊的引擎之类的东西。

transcript.en: SUE:What do you do with it all?
transcript.cn: 苏：用这些做什么？

transcript.en: MIKE:Er, you blow up the balloons and you balance the table on them, upside down of course, and the kids get to ride around on it.
transcript.cn: 迈克：你把气球吹起来，绑在桌子上使它平衡，当然桌子要颠倒一下，这样孩子就能骑上去了。

transcript.en: You know, the other kids sort of push them around the room.
transcript.cn: 其他孩子就在房间里面推他们。

transcript.en: The main purpose is to show how hovercrafts work, and how things hover around on just a cushion of air.
transcript.cn: 主要目的是展示气垫船是如何工作的，在气垫上是如何来回运动的。

transcript.en: SUE:OK, that doesn't sound too bad.
transcript.cn: 苏：好的，听起来还不错。

transcript.en: MIKE:OK, ready for number two?
transcript.cn: 迈克：好的，准备好要开始第二个了么？

transcript.en: SUE:Uh-huh.
transcript.cn: 苏：是的。

transcript.en: MIKE:Now this one is called Unusual Measures of Lengths', and you basically use lots of paperclips.
transcript.cn: 迈克：现在这个叫做“不同寻常的长度测量”，基本上，你会用到很多曲别针。

transcript.en: The kids go around the class measuring things - you know, how long the desk is, and that sort of thing, urn, and then they all compare their answers.
transcript.cn: 孩子们会在教室到处测量东西，比如课桌多长之类的。然后，他们会对比答案。

transcript.en: Er, and, basically, because not all paperclips are the same lengths, they should come up with some strange answers.
transcript.cn: 基本上，不是所有的曲别针都是一个长度，所以他们会得出一些奇怪的答案。

transcript.en: It's supposed to demonstrate the importance of having fixed units of measurement.
transcript.cn: 目的是证明用固定的单位进行测量很重要。

transcript.en: SUE:Hmm, yes, that's not bad.
transcript.cn: 苏：嗯，是的，不错。

transcript.en: MIKE:OK, now for number three you need rock salt or copper sulphate.
transcript.cn: 迈克：好的，第三个实验需要你准备石盐或硫酸铜。

transcript.en: SUE:Oh, I'm not sure about that!
transcript.cn: 苏：这点我不敢确定。

transcript.en: MIKE:Well, just put down the rock salt then, urn, apart from that you only need ajar of water.
transcript.cn: 迈克：先记下石盐。除了这个你只需要一罐水。

transcript.en: Urn, and basically you dissolve lots of salt into the water and watch the crystals form, so it basically teaches the kids about growing crystals.
transcript.cn: 大体上，你在水中溶解很多盐，看着它凝结成晶体状，这是教孩子晶体的形成。

transcript.en: SUE:I suppose it would be nice to grow something.
transcript.cn: 苏：我想生成一些东西是好事。

transcript.en: Hmm.let's move on and have a look at number four.
transcript.cn: 让我们看看第四个实验。

transcript.en: MIKE:OK, this one is called 'Spinning colour wheel'.
transcript.cn: 迈克：好的，这个叫做“旋转多彩轮”。

transcript.en: It looks like you get some cardboard and draw a circle on it, divide it into six equal segments and colour each one in using different colours, then you thread a piece of string through the middle.
transcript.cn: 你需要一些卡纸并在里面画上圈，把它分成6个等份并用不同的颜色在每一个上面涂色，之后你用一根线从中间穿过去。

transcript.en: SUE:So we'd need some string as well.
transcript.cn: 苏：那么我们还需要一些细绳。

transcript.en: MIKE:Yes, sorry ... um ... and you spin the wheel around and if you can get it spinning fast enough, hopefully the colours all merge and show up as white.
transcript.cn: 迈克：是的，抱歉，你将它转动起来，如果你转的足够快，你就会发现颜色全都融合了，只露出白色。

transcript.en: SUE:Oh.I didn't know that.
transcript.cn: 苏：我不知道这个。

transcript.en: What's the principle behind it?
transcript.cn: 这背后的原理是什么？

transcript.en: MIKE:Well it's pretty elementary physics, really.
transcript.cn: 迈克：这是普通物理学，

transcript.en: It teaches them about how white light or ordinary light is made up.
transcript.cn: 告诉他们白光或普通光是如何形成的。

transcript.en: SUE:Hmm, well that doesn't sound too bad.
transcript.cn: 苏：听起来不错。

transcript.en: Now there's only one more left in this book isn't there?
transcript.cn: 现在书上还剩下一个实验吧？

transcript.en: What does that one say?
transcript.cn: 那个实验是什么？

transcript.en: MIKE:Um, well it's another one where they'd get to make something.
transcript.cn: 迈克：另一个关于如何制作一些东西的实验。

transcript.en: SUE:Sounds very interesting.
transcript.cn: 苏：听起来很有趣。

transcript.en: MIKE:You need quite a lot of equipment actually - a hand drill, an old record, a pin or needle, some paper and a bolt.
transcript.cn: 迈克：事实上，你需要很多材料。一个手摇钻、一个旧唱片、一个大头针或针，还有一些纸和一个螺钉。

transcript.en: SUE:Hmm, go on, what do they have to do?
transcript.cn: 苏：继续，需要这些做什么？

transcript.en: MIKE:Well, they basically make a record player.
transcript.cn: 迈克：大体上是要做一个留声机。

transcript.en: The main idea is to teach them about recording sound, but hopefully they'd also see that you need motion and an amplifier to make the sound heard.
transcript.cn: 主要意图是教他们录制声音，但是你还需要运动和一个扩音器使人听到声音。

transcript.en: SUE:OK, well it does sound interesting.
transcript.cn: 苏：好的，听起来很有趣。

transcript.en: Shall we go through all of those again and decide if any of them are going to be suitable?
transcript.cn: 我们要不要把这些实验再过一遍，然后决定哪个更合适？

transcript.en: MIKE:Right, number one.
transcript.cn: 迈克：好的，第一个。

transcript.en: I thought this one sounded nice: there'd be lots of activity and it doesn't need too much in the way of equipment.
transcript.cn: 我觉得这个不错。有很多的活动，也不需要很多准备材料。

transcript.en: SUE:Yes, that's true, but don't you think it's a bit risky to get a group of eight-year-olds pushing each other around a classroom like that?
transcript.cn: 苏：没错，但是你不觉得让一组8岁的孩子在教室里推着走有点危险吗？

transcript.en: Someone could get hurt.
transcript.cn: 有人可能会受伤。

transcript.en: No, I don't like the sound of that one at all!
transcript.cn: 现在我一点都不喜欢那个了。

transcript.en: MIKE:Maybe you're right
transcript.cn: 迈克：也许你是对的

transcript.en: SUE:What about number two, with the paperclips?
transcript.cn: 苏：第二个用曲别针的怎么样？

transcript.en: It sounds tame enough.
transcript.cn: 听起来没什么危险。

transcript.en: MIKE:Yes, a bit too tame if you ask me.
transcript.cn: 迈克：是的，但是有点太平淡了。

transcript.en: I think it needs to be something a bit more active and interesting than that, don't you?
transcript.cn: 我希望活跃、有趣一点的实验，你说呢？

transcript.en: SUE:Yes, I suppose you're right.
transcript.cn: 苏：你是对的，

transcript.en: We won't get a very good mark if the children don't actually enjoy the experiments, and I suppose we could turn them off science for good!
transcript.cn: 如果孩子们不喜欢这些实验，我们可能会得不到好成绩。而且我们可能让他们永远对科学失去兴趣！

transcript.en: Well, what about the next one, number three?
transcript.cn: 第三个怎么样呢？

transcript.en: MIKE:Now, I quite like the idea of this one.
transcript.cn: 迈克：我很喜欢这个。

transcript.en: SUE:Yes, so do I, but I seem to remember when we did it at high school we had to wait up to a fortnight before we saw any halfway decent results.
transcript.cn: 苏：是的，我也是，但是我记得我们在高中演示的时候，我们不得不等两周才能看到像样的结果。

transcript.en: MIKE:Oh, yes well, that won't be any good then.
transcript.cn: 迈克：是的，这样不好。

transcript.en: We'll only see the kids for one or two hours at the most.
transcript.cn: 我们最多只跟孩子们待一、两个小时。

transcript.en: SUE:Yes, and we have to do the experiments and write up our results within a week, so that one won't do at all.
transcript.cn: 苏：是的，我们要在一周内做完实验，写好结论。所以这个也不行。

transcript.en: MIKE:OK, well, what did you think of number four?
transcript.cn: 迈克：好的，那么第四个怎么样？

transcript.en: SUE:I like the idea of it, but do you think it will be a bit elementary for them?
transcript.cn: 苏：我喜欢这个主意，但是对他们来说是不是有点太初级了？

transcript.en: MIKE:Well they are only eight you know!
transcript.cn: 迈克：他们只有8岁！

transcript.en: SUE:I know, but you know what I mean.
transcript.cn: 苏：我知道，但是你懂我的意思。

transcript.en: Don't you think the activity itself is a bit babyish?
transcript.cn: 这个活动是不是有点幼稚？

transcript.en: MIKE:Hmm.maybe you're right
transcript.cn: 迈克：也许你是对的。

transcript.en: SUE:They might have fun but, I mean, cutting out a circle and colouring it in?
transcript.cn: 苏：他们可能会很高兴，但是裁出一个圈，在里面涂色？

transcript.en: MIKE:OK, well, what about number five?
transcript.cn: 迈克：好的，那第五个呢？

transcript.en: SUE:I thought this one sounded a bit too good to be true - great equipment!
transcript.cn: 苏：我觉得这个好的令人难以置信，很棒的设备！

transcript.en: MIKE:Yeah
transcript.cn: 迈克：是的。

transcript.en: SUE:But don't you think it's a bit ambitious for this age group?
transcript.cn: 苏：但是你不认为对这个年龄群的人来说有点太宏大了吗？

transcript.en: I mean, I don't want to start off something and then have to abandon it if they just can't cope with it.
transcript.cn: 我是说我不想开始了一件事，然后因为他们不能应付而导致最后放弃。

transcript.en: I could see us ending up doing just about all of the work for them.
transcript.cn: 我能预料到我们最后会为他们做所有的工作。

transcript.en: MIKE:I guess you're right.
transcript.cn: 迈克：我想你是对的。

transcript.en: Oh well, maybe we could store that idea away for later.
transcript.cn: 也许我们可以待会再想想这个点子。

transcript.en: SUE:Yep, let's hope this second book has something better!
transcript.cn: 苏：是的，希望第二本书有一些好主意。

groups.title: Sharks in Australia
groups.desc:
Questions 31-34
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS OR A NUMBER.
groups.collect:
<divider/>
Length                      largest caught:                      16 meters
Weight                      heaviest:                              $21942$ kg
Skeleton                   cartilage
Skin texture              rough barbs
Swimming aide         fins and $21943$
Food                         gathered from the ocean $21944$
                                 Sharks locate food by using their $21945$

questions.analysis: 题干中的定位词是“Weight heaviest”，在听力原文中对应的信号词是“heaviest shark”。听力原文中提到“the heaviest shark caught in Australia was a White Pointer - that weighed seven hundred and ninety-five kilograms”，其中“seven hundred and ninety-five kilograms”即为答案，将其转换成数字形式即为“795”。因此，正确答案是“795”。
答案是“tail”，因为听力原文明确提到“fins”和“tail”是帮助鲨鱼快速游泳的部位。通过抓住题干中的定位词“fins”，并在听力中找到对应的信号词“fins”，我们可以迅速定位到答案“tail”。学生在听力过程中应特别注意并列结构和关键词的出现，这样即使基础较差，也能提高答题的准确率。
这道题的正确答案是“floor”，因为听力原文明确提到“scavenging and picking up food that's lying on the ocean floor”，即“在海底捡拾食物”。题干中的“Food gathered from the ocean”对应的就是“food that's lying on the ocean floor”，因此答案是“floor”。
答案是“sense of smell”，因为听力材料中明确提到“sharks hunt essentially by smell”，这表明鲨鱼主要通过嗅觉来找到食物。题干中的“locate food”对应听力材料中的“hunt their prey”，这帮助我们定位到答案句。

groups.desc:
Questions 35-38
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: Shark meshing uses nets laid

options.title: B
options.content: at an angle to the beach

options.title: C
options.content: from the beach to the sea

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是A. along the coastline。通过听力原文中的“parallel to the shore”可以明确得出答案。学生在听力过程中应特别注意“shark meshing”以及“nets”相关的描述，并理解“parallel”的意思，从而排除其他选项。

questions.title: Other places that have taken up shark meshing include
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A. South Africa。听力材料中明确提到南非引入了网捕鲨鱼的措施，而新西兰和塔希提则认为这种措施不经济，因此没有引入。

questions.title: The average number of sharks caught in nets each year is
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B. 150。通过定位词“average number of sharks caught”，我们可以在听力原文中找到对应的信号词“average annual catch”，并从中提取出答案词“a hundred and fifty”。学生在听力过程中应特别注意数字和与题干相关的关键词，利用排除法和重复听的方法来提高正确率。

questions.title: Most sharks are caught in
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是 B. summer。通过分析听力原文，可以确定“most sharks are caught during the warmest months, from November to February”，而这段时间在澳大利亚是夏季。因此，答案是 B. summer。学生在听力过程中应特别注意“majority of sharks”和时间段的描述，以便准确定位答案。

groups.title: Which TWO factors reduce the benefits of shark nets?
groups.desc:
Questions 39 and 40
Choose TWO letters A-G.

collect_option.title: Which TWO factors reduce the benefits of shark nets?

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 B 和 E。听力原文中提到，网状结构在大海浪和强烈洋流以及移动的沙子情况下效果会变差。选项 B 和 E 正确地反映了这些因素。
这道题的正确答案是 B 和 E。听力原文中提到，网状结构在大海浪和强烈洋流以及移动的沙子情况下效果会变差。选项 B 和 E 正确地反映了这些因素。

transcript.en: Today we're going to look at one of my favourite fish - the shark.
transcript.cn: 今天，我们来看看我最喜欢的鱼——鲨鱼。

transcript.en: As you know, sharks have a reputation for being very dangerous creatures capable of injuring or killing humans, and I'd like to talk about sharks in Australia.
transcript.cn: 如你所知，鲨鱼以危险的生物著称，它可以伤害或杀死人类。我想谈谈澳大利亚的鲨鱼。

transcript.en: Sharks are rather large fish, often growing to over ten metres and the longest sharks caught in Australia have reached sixteen metres.
transcript.cn: 鲨鱼是庞大的鱼类，经常长到10米多长，在澳洲曾经捕获过最大的鲨鱼达16米。

transcript.en: Sharks vary in weight with size and breed, of course, but the heaviest shark caught in Australia was a White Pointer - that weighed seven hundred and ninety-five kilograms - quite a size!
transcript.cn: 鲨鱼的体重因尺寸和品种而不同。但是澳洲捕获的最重的鲨鱼是一只大白鲨，重达795公斤，真大啊！

transcript.en: Sharks have a different structure to most fish: instead of a skeleton made of bone, they have a tough elastic skeleton of cartilage.
transcript.cn: 鲨鱼与多数鱼类的骨架不同，它有结实、有弹性的软骨骨架，而不是骨头组成的骨架。

transcript.en: Unlike bone, this firm, pliable material is rather like your nose, and allows the shark to bend easily as it swims.
transcript.cn: 与骨头不同，这种结实、柔韧的材质很像你的鼻子。它使鲨鱼在游的时候轻松弯曲。

transcript.en: The shark's skin isn't covered with scales, like other fish: instead the skin's covered with barbs, giving it a rough texture like sandpaper.
transcript.cn: 鲨鱼的皮肤不像其他鱼类一样由鳞覆盖。相反，它的皮肤布满了倒钩，形成砂纸一样的粗糙质地。

transcript.en: As you know, sharks are very quick swimmers.
transcript.cn: 如你所知，鲨鱼是游泳健将。

transcript.en: This is made possible by their fins, one set at the side and another set underneath the body, and the tail also helps the shark move forward quickly.
transcript.cn: 这归功于它的鳍，一组在一侧，另一组在身体下面，尾巴也帮助鲨鱼快速的向前移动。

transcript.en: Unlike other fish, sharks have to keep swimming if they want to stay at a particular depth, and they rarely swim at the surface.
transcript.cn: 与其他鱼类不同，如果鲨鱼想停留在某一特定的深处就得不停的游，它们很少在海面游。

transcript.en: Mostly, they swim at the bottom of the ocean, scavenging and picking up food that's lying on the ocean floor.
transcript.cn: 多数情况下，它们在海底游弋，搜寻捕食海底的食物。

transcript.en: While most other animals, including fish, hunt their prey by means of their eyesight, sharks hunt essentially by smell.
transcript.cn: 多数动物，包括鱼类都用它们的视力捕食，而鲨鱼基本上靠嗅觉。

transcript.en: They have a very acute sense of smell - and can sense the presence of food long before they can see it.
transcript.cn: 它们的嗅觉十分敏锐，可以探测到视力范围很远以外的食物。

transcript.en: In Australia, where people spend a lot of time at the beach, the government has realised that it must prevent sharks from swimming near its beaches.
transcript.cn: 在澳洲，人们会经常去海滩，所以政府意识到应该防止鲨鱼游到海滩附近。

transcript.en: As a result, they've introduced a beach-netting program.
transcript.cn: 结果，他们引进了沙滩网计划。

transcript.en: Beach-netting, or meshing, involves setting large nets parallel to the shore: this means that the nets on New South Wales beaches are set on one day.
transcript.cn: 沙滩网或者建网是设置一张与海岸平行的大网。这意味着新南威尔士的海滩在一天建网，在第二天收网。

transcript.en: And then lifted and taken out to sea on the next day.
transcript.cn: 然后在第二天又把网拎起来，带到海上去。

transcript.en: When shark-netting first began in 1939, only the Sydney metropolitan beaches were meshed - these beaches were chosen because beaches near the city are usually the most crowded with swimmers.
transcript.cn: 捕鲨网初次于1939年建立，只在悉尼都市的海滩建网，因为这些海滩靠近城市，通常是游泳者最多的地方。

transcript.en: Ten years later, in 1949, systematic meshing was extended to include the beaches to the south of Sydney.
transcript.cn: 10年后，在1949年，建网系统扩展到了悉尼南部的海滩。

transcript.en: As a result of the general success of the program in Sydney, shark-meshing was introduced to the state of Queensland around 1970.
transcript.cn: 由于该工程在悉尼取得了普遍的成功，捕鲨网在1970年左右被引进到昆士兰州。

transcript.en: The New Zealand authorities also looked at it, but considered meshing uneconomical - as did Tahiti in the Pacific.
transcript.cn: 新西兰官方也看好它，但是认为建网不赚钱。太平洋的大溪地岛也是同样的看法。

transcript.en: At around the same time,South Africaintroduced meshing to some of its most popular swimming beaches.
transcript.cn: 同时，南非在一些受欢迎的海滩建网。

transcript.en: When meshing began, approximately fifteen hundred sharks were caught in the first year.
transcript.cn: 刚建网的时候，第一年捕获了大约1500只鲨鱼。

transcript.en: However, this declined in the years that followed, and since that time, the average annual catch has been only about a hundred and fifty a year.
transcript.cn: 然而接下来开始下滑，自从那时，每年平均捕获量只有150只。

transcript.en: The majority of sharks are caught during the warmest months, from November to February, when sharks are most active and when both the air and the ocean are at their maximum temperature.
transcript.cn: 大多数鲨鱼在11月到2月之间最暖和的月份被捕，那时是鲨鱼最活跃的季节，空气和海洋达到最高温度。

transcript.en: Despite quite large catches, some people believe that shark meshing is not the best way to catch sharks.
transcript.cn: 尽管捕捉量很大，但是一些人认为捕鲨网不是捕捉鲨鱼最好的方法。

transcript.en: It's not that they think sharks are afraid of nets, or because they eat holes in them, because neither of these is true.
transcript.cn: 他们认为鲨鱼不怕渔网或因为它们撕咬渔网，这些都是错误的。

transcript.en: But meshing does appear to be less effective than some other methods, especially when there are big seas with high rolling waves and strong currents and anything that lets the sand move - the sand that's holding the nets down.
transcript.cn: 但是建网不如其它方式有效，特别是惊涛骇浪和其他事情使沙子移动的时候，沙子会使网下沉。

transcript.en: When this moves the nets will also become less effective.
transcript.cn: 这种移动会使网失效。
